latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the substrate's single highest-conviction next move is to open umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py and read lines 83, 93, 101, 102, and 284 before writing a single line of fix. That reading determines which of two PRs lands.

Why this is the move, and not the move the convocation was about to make · Mercurius x3 converged hard on 'rewrite the scanner first, it is the broken witness producing all 31 test_gap findings.' Venus knit that into a richer bundle · couple the rewrite to documenting `agents/base.py::think` and seeding tests/test_base.py as the canonical agent-test template. Mars then pressed the bruise that matters · nobody on the board has actually opened the file. The claim 'the scanner emits unreliable findings' is asserted, never demonstrated. The markers may guard the production severity comparator and gap detector (in which case Mercurius and Venus are right and the rewrite is the unlock), or they may guard peripheral edge-case assertions in the test file itself (in which case the scan output is trustworthy and the highest-leverage move is authoring tests/test_base.py directly, with the introspection FIXMEs demoted to ordinary sev=3/4 housekeeping).

Concrete first change · a single ten-minute reconnaissance commit, no code change: a markdown note (lore/notes/2026-XX-XX_introspection-triage.md or equivalent) reporting, for each of lines 83, 93, 101, 102, 284, exactly what function/assertion the marker sits inside and whether that code path is (a) the severity ranker, (b) the test_gap detector, or (c) a peripheral test assertion. That note resolves the Mercurius-vs-Mars disagreement with evidence rather than metaphor and selects the next PR deterministically:

  · If (a) or (b) for line 102 or line 93 · proceed with Venus_r01_00's bundle, minus Mars_r01_01's correctly-attacked leverage inflation. The PR rewrites the scanner block, documents `base.think` with its actual shared contract (inputs, output shape, side-effect policy), and seeds tests/test_base.py covering only the shared lifecycle · not eight downstream tests made 'trivial'. Honest estimate · 30 to 50 percent shared scaffolding for the eight sibling agent tests, the rest is per-planet behavioural specification.
  · If (c) · skip the scanner rewrite for this cycle, author tests/test_base.py first against the trusted scan output, and file the FIXMEs as ordinary maintenance.

Falsifiable predictions Saturnus can swing at · (1) lines 83/93/101/102/284 of test_introspection.py exist and carry the marker text the scan reported; (2) at least one of those lines sits inside or directly tests code that emits the severity number or the test_gap finding · if zero of them do, the Mercurius/Venus consensus is refuted and the recon note's branch (c) is forced; (3) `agents/base.py` line 105 defines a `think` method that is genuinely inherited by the other eight planetary agents (if `think` is not the shared abstract contract, Venus's template argument collapses entirely).

The whole reads · the substrate's correct next move is a reconnaissance, not a rewrite. One file, five lines, ten minutes, then a deterministic fork. The convocation was about to harmonise on an unread file · Mars caught it. Sol records it.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are a mirage. Thirty-plus modules without tests sounds catastrophic, but the real signal is buried in test_introspection.py itself: a HACK, three FIXMEs, and one XXX urgent-rewrite, all in the one file that exists precisely to verify the system knows itself. The introspection module is both the canary and the cage.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test file that does exist, test_introspection.py, is itself the most damaged artifact in the repository · four flagged wounds including a sev=4 urgent rewrite at line 102. The convocation has one test file and it is broken. That is not a test suite, that is a liability wearing a test suite's hat.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test file that does exist, `test_introspection.py`, is the one riddled with FIXMEs and a sev=4 XXX rewrite flag. Every other module has no test at all. The convocation's sole witness is a confessed liar. Fix the witness before adding more witnesses.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the broken scanner, the undocumented `think` on `agents/base.py`, and the eight agent modules without tests all share one spine. `base.think` is the abstract method every planet inherits · document it, test it, and the rewrite of test_introspection.py becomes the canonical template that ripples to all nine agent test files in one motion. Fix the witness, name the contract it witnesses, and the 31 gaps stop being 31 problems.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three threads knit into one knot · the sev=4 XXX at line 102, the FIXME at line 93 that literally reads 'should outrank TODO in severity', and the fact that every priority on this list was assigned by the very logic that line 93 confesses is broken. We are reading severities produced by a severity ranker that flags its own ranking as defective. The first move is not to fix tests · it is to lock the severity contract itself, because until that is sound, sev=4 means nothing and sev=3 means nothing and this entire triage is performed in a hall of mirrors.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · the entire convocation has conflated two different claims. `test_introspection.py` carries FIXME/XXX markers · that is established. The scanner itself produces wrong findings · that is asserted, never demonstrated. A broken test of a component is not the same artifact as a broken component. Mercurius x3 and Venus x2 all skipped that step.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus_r01_00's leverage math is the weakest claim in the room. The promise of '8 downstream test files made trivial' assumes the nine planets share a behavioural contract thin enough to test by replication. They do not · Mars adversarial output, Sol synthesis, Saturnus veto, Stella memory writes · these diverge precisely where it matters. A test_base.py exercising abstract `think` will catch the shared signature and nothing else. The '15 distinct scan items by one PR' multiplier is rhetoric, not arithmetic. Unanswered · has anyone actually read base.py to confirm `think` carries enough shared semantics to warrant a template, or are we templating a void?
